annotations.text: The constellation of findings—particularly the combination of albinism, pulmonary fibrosis, bronchiectasis, platelet dysfunction, and a family history of similar respiratory disease in siblings with albinism—strongly suggests a rare, inherited disorder rather than a common acquired pulmonary condition.

The most compelling diagnostic hypothesis is **Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome (HPS)**, a rare autosomal recessive disorder of lysosomal trafficking that belongs to the group of oculocutaneous albinism syndromes.

The pulmonary manifestation typically presents in the second to fourth decade with dyspnea, cough, and progressive restrictive lung disease, often accompanied by bronchiectasis and honeycombing on high-resolution CT—exactly as described in this case.

The development of pulmonary hypertension, right ventricular dilation, and elevated right ventricular systolic pressure (56 mm Hg) further supports the progression of this fibrotic interstitial lung disease to cor pulmonale, a known terminal complication of HPS.
While infectious, inflammatory, and environmental etiologies must be considered, the chronicity, early onset, multisystem involvement, and familial pattern point decisively toward a genetic syndrome.
The presence of clubbing, decreased lung volumes, ground-glass opacities, and architectural distortion are classic radiographic hallmarks of HPS-related pulmonary fibrosis.
The fact that his siblings also have albinism and milder respiratory problems strongly supports an autosomal recessive inheritance pattern, which is typical of HPS.

The combination of albinism, platelet dysfunction, pulmonary fibrosis, and familial occurrence is virtually diagnostic of Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome, particularly subtype HPS-1 or HPS-4, which are most commonly associated with pulmonary fibrosis.
the patient’s Puerto Rican ancestry further strengthens this diagnosis.
The patient’s history of chronic productive cough, wheezing, and bronchiectasis, initially misattributed to asthma or infection, is also consistent with HPS.
